vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but fails to account for the severe decline of the specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor, but the foliage is in advanced necrosis. P1 (String of Nickels) is present but sparse. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are effectively absent or reduced to non-viable remnants.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No viable silver guest detected)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot; foliage is minimal and appears dehydrated.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot; no evidence of Mexican Mint; soil surface is barren.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot; white rabbit anchor present; one large leaf is necrotic/yellowing, one small leaf remains.
plant_audit.P4: Black pot; no evidence of Silver Guest; soil surface is barren.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dry, granular, and lacking organic vitality.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite/egg-shell fragments present; no fungal growth observed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-5: Negligible change; baseline state of decline established.
temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-1: Progressive yellowing of the large Pothos leaf in P3; soil moisture appears to have decreased.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Stasis; no new growth; Pothos leaf in P3 shows increased chlorosis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The Pothos (P3) is suffering from severe chlorosis and likely root-zone failure. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) show no signs of active growth and are effectively dormant or dead.
vision_report.anomalies: None detected; the lack of growth is the primary anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is failing. The Pothos in P3 is the only specimen showing significant structural presence, though it is clearly dying. The other pots are essentially empty of the registered species, suggesting a total loss of the intended biome. The environment is static and lacks the necessary moisture or nutrient support for recovery.
